transcript: Sponsored by surfshark early in the Smartwatch Wars a company called mob voy carved out a following with a wearable called The Tick watch a smart low profile timepiece controlled by a clever capacitive touch drip on the side even after ditching its homegrown tickware platform for Google's wear OS montvoy kept the attention of enthusiasts because every year it was among the first to use qualcomm's newest wearable chipsets and it was the only manufacturer outside of Casio offering a dual layer display to help with battery life and daylight visibility the ticlatch Pro 5 continues that tradition of Hardware Excellence but it launches into a wear OS world that's oddly more fragmented than ever and it debuts to an audience more skeptical than ever of mobboy's commitment to its products is it worth paying the same as a pixel watch will cost you I'm Michael Fisher and it's time to find out [Music] straddling the Divide as they do between fashion and Tech smart watches are bound by inherently subjective Aesthetics so let me start with a subjective opinion I don't think my boy will ever make a watch that resonates with my particular sense of style The Tick watch Pro 5 is fine with a lowercase f the company wants me to call it obsidian but what it really is is a dull utterly unremarkable matte black yes there's a knurled surface treatment to the bezel and there's a slight flare to the lugs and finally a digital Crown to navigate the interface but it's 24 millimeter band and keeper are stamped from generic silicone that screams 10 on Amazon on the wrist its lightweight case feels as forgettable as it looks and the only concession to color on the whole thing is the Crown's Red Ring which has Digital Trends anti-box all rightly points out reads as little more than a meaningless nod to the Apple watch now if you're one of the millions of minimalists shelling out for blank hoodies to hang in monochromatic closets or you're a technopragmatist who never looks past a stacked spec sheet well this will make you happy Qualcomm has spent years promising that its next chipset will solve all the problems with Android wearables and the Snapdragon W5 plus gen 1 is as powerful as it is awkwardly named by the same token Google promised year after year that is next wearable platform would finally fix everything well where OS is no more visually arresting than it was when I first showed it off last summer on that Montblanc Summit 3.The Play Store is still weirdly buggy voice dictation still fails more often than it hits and hilariously the Google Assistant still won't run on any watch not made by Samsung or Google on the bright side I can't find fault in version 3.5's performance opening apps is faster than ever and there's no lag when you're scrubbing through notifications with each turn of the crown punctuated by a pleasant haptic click what's more of the aluminum and Gorilla Glass fiberglass and nylon case is rated to five atmospheres of water resistance and tested to mil standard 810h for sand ice shock and vibration and it packs within it a 628 milliamp hour battery folks that's more than double the size of the cell in the pixel watch bigger even than the battery in Samsung's Galaxy watch 5 Pro comparing that monster power pack with the power efficient processor was already going to do wonders for uptime but remember that the tick watch also spends most of the day with its power hungry AMOLED watch face turned off above it sits the ultra low power or ulp display that stays on and sips milliamps and while earlier models gave you a little more than the time this one shows you the day date steps seconds Bluetooth and NFC status and and you can spin the crown for more heart rate calorie burn even a compass all accessible without ever waking up the OLED underneath there's a separate backlight just for the ulp you can set it to any one of 18 colors and get this if you use the watch in exercise mode the backlight color can be set to change automatically based on your heart rate I think that's very clever on the subject of Health tracking tick exercise is one of count them 15 mob voy apps pre-loaded onto the watch probably the most flagrant invasion of bloatware ever to hit my wrist frankly I don't often have use for this much Fitness granularity in my wearables and on the rare occasions I do like for that e-bike review I recently wrote 100 miles for well I'm just more likely to trust an established player like Garmin Garmin doesn't pester me by reminding me to stand up twice an hour like it's 2015.It doesn't give me duplicate calendar notifications for some reason it tracks my sleep reliably unlike the tick watch which recently missed logging three hours of sleep and Garmin also knows how to build an app that looks well not like this oh and while I'm giving feedback it's excellent that I can make and take calls from the watch and they're pretty clear too if a little on the quiet side but it is a bummer there isn't a second Pusher I can reassign to whatever I want the second button on the watch is only for recent apps the uplifting conclusion to that Downer of a detour is that I get to finish my point about battery life put simply it's the best I've seen on a wear OS watch after severing its magnetic umbilical at noon on Saturday the thing didn't give up the ghost until sometime on Thursday night that's over five days of battery in the world of Ware OS where you're charging the pixel watch every night and most of Samsung's Galaxy watches every other that's incredible endurance it means if I forget the charger or just as critically a wall wart that supports the USB type a connector my boy insists on sticking with I can still make it more than a weekend and it means I'm not stuck with the out of box watch faces because I can use a third-party solution like facer that uses the new silicon to enable some fun 3D effects and I don't need to worry that it's killing the battery again watches are deeply personal objects and personally this form over function Stan would rather buy something with a bit more panache now normally people like me would be counterbalanced by the more pragmatic flavor of geek but mobboy recently dealt a massive blow to its standing with those Folks by promising and so far failing to deliver a Ware OS 3 update to its earlier watches putting them over a half year behind competitors like fossil for the same update the company maintains those upgrades are still coming but until they actually land it's easy to see why mobboy's user reviews are in the toilet don't buy based on promises and all that I'd love to see a world where mobboy gives up on its bespoke software Ambitions doubles down on software updates and uses the money it saves to hire some veteran watch designers because there's so much good in the ticwatch Pro 5.And for 350 bucks I do think it's a good value but that's mostly due to prices that in my opinion are higher than they should be overall in the Ware OS ecosystem and with Samsung's Next Generation due later this summer and a pixel Watch 2 rumored to fix most of what's broken on the first one well I agree with Victoria's song at the verge timing is everything if you're tired of hearing about surf shark I get it after all the company's been my sponsor for four years but if you think about it that's a good thing it means surfshark has done its job of keeping me and its customers safe while browsing the web all over the world you also might have noticed that I haven't taken a sponsorship from any other VPN company since I started and that's not because I can't because I haven't felt the need to use anyone but surf shark in addition to securing my connection on Wi-Fi networks all around the world it also guarantees I can still access media and services that might otherwise be restricted by the country I'm browsing from if that sounds useful to you get surf shark at the link in the description and use code Mr mobile for 83 off and three extra months free thanks to surfshark for sponsoring this video this video was produced after several weeks with two tick watch profile review samples provided by mobboy of course as always the company got no early preview of this video in fact it had no editorial input at all and these conclusions are mine and mine alone Please Subscribe if you'd like to see more videos like this and check out my review of that Garmin Mark Captain Gen 2 from Hawaii and the Google pixel watch before that until next time from Michael Fisher thanks for watching and stay mobile my friends [Music]
